entries.description: Undeniably funky techno.

entries.title: Jump Source - Fold
entries.description: Priori and Patrick Holland have been on a tear, but their debut LP as Jump Source hits harder. It's one of the sharpest, best-built electronic albums you'll hear this year.

entries.title: Loukeman - SD-3
entries.description: A restless collage of folk, pop, R&B and rap, stitched into emotionally charged electronics.

entries.description: A slow-burn of deep grooves from a Chicago community staple.

entries.title: Oxfam launches collaborative protest track featuring artists from Palestine, DRC and South Sudan

entries.title: Australia's Soma Festival reveals 2026 lineup

entries.title: A new tool allows artists to check whether their tracks are used in AI
entries.description: AI Watchdog stems from an investigation from The Atlantic into AI data training.

entries.title: james K - Friend Remixes
entries.description: One of last year's best albums is recast into a set of aqueous club jams, anchored throughout by the precision of james K's voice.

entries.description: The Drain Gang mainstay's latest LP takes on big-room trance with aplomb.

entries.description: The Berlin producer picks up where his 2018 LP Gens left off, wringing extreme feeling from techno's austere templates.

entries.description: The Scottish promoter will host three parties in Glasgow, Edinburgh and Bristol.

entries.title: Jasmín - Bite The Hand That Feeds You
